request.question: What does Sam Weir and Gold Coast, Queensland have in common?

title: Gold Coast, Queensland
Upper Coomera, Queensland
TAFE Queensland Gold Coast
Sam Weir (endurance athlete)
South East Queensland
Female Gridiron League of Queensland
The Arts Centre Gold Coast
Gold Coast Art Prize
Gold Coast Blaze
Gold Coast Film Festival
sentences: The Gold Coast is a coastal area in the Australian state of Queensland, approximately 66 km south-southeast of the state capital Brisbane and immediately north of the border with New South Wales.
With a census-estimated 2016 population of 638,090, The Gold Coast is the sixth-largest city in Australia, making it the largest non-capital city, and the second-largest city in Queensland.
Upper Coomera is a suburb on the Gold Coast in Queensland, Australia.
Upper Coomera and Coomera have long been the main centre of urban development on the Gold Coast and are considered to be, along with Southport and Robina, one of the Gold Coast's three urban centres.
Upper Coomera is a heavily suburbanised suburb consisting of many large residential developments and commercial centres.

Despite already being heavily developed and having an extraordinarily large population, Upper Coomera is predicted by both the Queensland Government and Gold Coast City Council to grow and develop at an exponential rate well into the next decade.

Upper Coomera is a popular place of residence for dual-city commuters as it is placed roughly halfway between the central commercial districts of Southport and Beenleigh and within reasonable travelling distance to Brisbane CBD.

TAFE Queensland Gold Coast (formally known at Gold Coast Institute of TAFE or GCIT) is a vocational education and training (VET) organisation on the Gold Coast region of Queensland, Australia, with five campuses across Southport and Coomera, Ashmore and Coolangatta.
It is the largest in the region offering over 300 courses to more than 16,000 students annually.
TAFE Queensland Gold Coast is part of the TAFE Queensland network which is the largest and most experienced training provider in the State, delivering technical education for more than 130 years.
TAFE Queensland provides services to more than 180,000 students each year - more than all of Queensland's universities combined.
Sam "Sammy" Weir (born 1990 in the Gold Coast, Australia) is an Australian endurance athlete.
He is known for undertaking some of the Australia's most challenging endurance events in inhospitable terrain.

South East Queensland (SEQ) is a bio-geographical, political, and administrative region of the state of Queensland in Australia, which contains 3.4 million people out of the state's population of 4.8 million.

The area covered by South East Queensland varies, depending on the definition of the region, though it tends to include Queensland's three largest cities: the capital city Brisbane; the Gold Coast; and the Sunshine Coast.

Its most common use is for political purposes, and covers 22420 km2 and incorporates 11 local government areas, extending 240 km from Noosa in the north to the Gold Coast and New South Wales border in the south (some sources include Tweed Heads (NSW) which is contiguous as an urban area with Brisbane/Gold Coast), and 140 km west to Toowoomba (which is simultaneously considered part of the Darling Downs region).
The Female Gridiron League of Queensland (FGLQ), the first league of its kind in Australia, is a full kit, women's tackle American nine-a-side football competition that commenced on August 24, 2012.
The FGLQ was founded by the Logan City Gridiron Football Club and is sanctioned by Gridiron Queensland.
Three teams participated in the first season in 2012, the Logan City Jets, Kenmore Panthers and Gold Coast Sea Wolves.
The first officially sanctioned game of women's gridiron was played between the Kenmore Panthers and Gold Coast Sea Wolves on August 24, 2012 at Logan Metro Sports, Browns Plains, Queensland.
The Logan City Jets defeated the Kenmore Panthers 38-20 in the Summerbowl I championship game played on November 2, 2012.

On March 23, 2013 the Female Gridiron League of Queensland Allstars, a team consisting of players who played in the 2012 FGLQ season, toured to Croydon, Melbourne to play the Western Foxes the first women's American Football team in Victoria.
The FGLQ Allstars won the game 28-14.
Four teams participated in the 2013 FGLQ season which commenced on September 6, 2013; Logan City Jets, Kenmore Panthers, Gold Coast Stingrays (formally Sea Wolves) and new team the Western Jaguars.

Following on from the pioneering efforts of the FGLQ other states across Australia established women's gridiron leagues including Gridiron NSW in 2013, Gridiron Victoria in 2013 and ACT Gridiron in 2014.
The Arts Centre Gold Coast (formerly Gold Coast Arts Centre) is a set of facilities that sits on the banks of the Nerang River in the Gold Coast in Queensland, Australia.
A large jetty makes the Arts Centre accessible by Gold Coast Ferries.
The art centre is also home to the Gold Coast City Art Gallery.
The Gold Coast Art Prize is an annual acquisitive exhibition run by the Gold Coast City Art Gallery in the city of the Gold Coast, Queensland, Australia.

One of Australia's oldest art prizes, it began in 1968 as the Gold Coast Art Prize but was known as the Conrad Jupiters Art Prize from 1990 to 2006 and as the Stan and Maureen Duke Gold Coast Prize from 2007 to 2011.
It has since reverted to its original name.
The Gold Coast Blaze was an Australian men's professional basketball team which competed in the National Basketball League (NBL).
The Blaze competed in their inaugural season in 2007/08.
The club was based on the Gold Coast and joined two other NBL clubs competing from Queensland.
The Blaze played its home games at the Gold Coast Convention and Exhibition Centre (GCCEC) in Broadbeach, known as "The Furnace" within the NBL.
The team was not related to the previous Gold Coast representative in the NBL, the Gold Coast Rollers.
The Gold Coast Film Festival is an annual film festival held across the Gold Coast in Queensland, Australia.
The festival is a celebration of film culture that delivers a program of feature films, special events, seminars and workshops, special guests and art.
The Gold Coast Film Festival’s purpose is to emerge the dynamic and creative culture community and to encourage the next generation of homegrown filmmakers.
The Gold Coast Film Festival celebrates and promotes all aspects of film and filmmaking from one of Australia’s pre-eminent filmmaking locations and holiday destinations – the Gold Coast.

The festival offers a culturally diverse program of films such as genre films, internationally acclaimed dramas, Japanese anime, local independent films and national and international special guests within the film industry.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: What does Sam Weir and Gold Coast, Queensland have in common?
hop1_docs: Sam Weir (endurance athlete) | Sam "Sammy" Weir (born 1990 in the Gold Coast, Australia) is an Australian endurance athlete.  He is known for undertaking some of the Australia's most challenging endurance events in inhospitable terrain.

Gold Coast Titans | The Gold Coast Titans are a professional rugby league football club, based on the Gold Coast, Queensland, Australia.  The club competes in the National Rugby League (NRL) premiership, Australia's elite rugby league competition.  It is the youngest of the sixteen clubs in the league, having commenced its inaugural season on 18 March 2007.  Since 2008, the Titans have played their home games at Cbus Super Stadium in Robina, Queensland.

Upper Coomera, Queensland | Upper Coomera is a suburb on the Gold Coast in Queensland, Australia.  Upper Coomera and Coomera have long been the main centre of urban development on the Gold Coast and are considered to be, along with Southport and Robina, one of the Gold Coast's three urban centres.  Upper Coomera is a heavily suburbanised suburb consisting of many large residential developments and commercial centres.  Despite already being heavily developed and having an extraordinarily large population, Upper Coomera is predicted by both the Queensland Government and Gold Coast City Council to grow and develop at an exponential rate well into the next decade.  Upper Coomera is a popular place of residence for dual-city commuters as it is placed roughly halfway between the central commercial districts of Southport and Beenleigh and within reasonable travelling distance to Brisbane CBD.

South East Queensland | South East Queensland (SEQ) is a bio-geographical, political, and administrative region of the state of Queensland in Australia, which contains 3.4 million people out of the state's population of 4.8 million.  The area covered by South East Queensland varies, depending on the definition of the region, though it tends to include Queensland's three largest cities: the capital city Brisbane; the Gold Coast; and the Sunshine Coast.  Its most common use is for political purposes, and covers 22420 km2 and incorporates 11 local government areas, extending 240 km from Noosa in the north to the Gold Coast and New South Wales border in the south (some sources include Tweed Heads (NSW) which is contiguous as an urban area with Brisbane/Gold Coast), and 140 km west to Toowoomba (which is simultaneously considered part of the Darling Downs region).

TAFE Queensland Gold Coast | TAFE Queensland Gold Coast (formally known at Gold Coast Institute of TAFE or GCIT) is a vocational education and training (VET) organisation on the Gold Coast region of Queensland, Australia, with five campuses across Southport and Coomera, Ashmore and Coolangatta.  It is the largest in the region offering over 300 courses to more than 16,000 students annually.  TAFE Queensland Gold Coast is part of the TAFE Queensland network which is the largest and most experienced training provider in the State, delivering technical education for more than 130 years.  TAFE Queensland provides services to more than 180,000 students each year - more than all of Queensland's universities combined.
Football Gold Coast | Football Gold Coast is a Football Queensland administrative zone encompassing Gold Coast, Queensland.

Mount Crosby Weir | The Mount Crosby Weir is a weir on the Brisbane River at Mount Crosby in South East Queensland, Australia.  The project was instigated by John Petrie at the end of the 19th century.  The town of Brisbane was expanding and seeking more reliable sources of drinking water than Enoggera Dam and Gold Creek Dam could provide.
response.summary_1: Passage [1] states Sam "Sammy" Weir was "born ... in the Gold Coast, Australia" and is an Australian endurance athlete. Passages [2] and [4] identify the Gold Coast as a place in Queensland, Australia (e.g., "The Gold Coast are ... based on the Gold Coast, Queensland, Australia" and "the Gold Coast ... in the state of Queensland in Australia"). Bridge entity: "the Gold Coast" — the next hop should confirm that the "Gold Coast, Australia" referenced for Sam Weir is the same entity as "Gold Coast, Queensland" (i.e., that his birthplace is the Gold Coast, Queensland). Use anchors: quote or search for "born ... in the Gold Coast, Australia" (Sam Weir) and "Gold Coast ... in the state of Queensland in Australia" (Gold Coast / Gold Coast Titans / South East Queensland) to retrieve an explicit identity link.
response.hop2_query: Sam Weir "born in the Gold Coast, Australia" "Gold Coast, Queensland"
hop2_docs: Sam Weir (endurance athlete) | Sam "Sammy" Weir (born 1990 in the Gold Coast, Australia) is an Australian endurance athlete.  He is known for undertaking some of the Australia's most challenging endurance events in inhospitable terrain.

Tennis Gold Coast | Tennis Gold Coast is the governing body for the sport of tennis in the Gold Coast, Queensland.  The organisation is based in Southport, Queensland in Australia.  Famous players from the Gold Coast are players such as Samantha Stosur and Bernard Tomic.
Gold Coast Stars FC | The Gold Coast Stars was an Australian football club from Gold Coast, Queensland, Australia who played in the Queensland State League.

Gold Coast Parklands | The Gold Coast Parklands was a complex serving the grey hound and harness racing industries on the Gold Coast, Queensland, Australia.  It opened on 7 December 1988 on the corner of Smith Street and Parklands Drive, Southport on the Gold Coast, Queensland at a cost of nine million dollars.

The Star Gold Coast | The Star Gold Coast is a casino and hotel located in the suburb of Broadbeach on the Gold Coast in Queensland, Australia.  The casino, which was Queensland's first, is situated next to the Nerang River and is operated by Star Entertainment Group.  It opened in February 1986 as Conrad Jupiters.  In 2017 it was rebranded as The Star, Gold Coast.

The Arts Centre Gold Coast | The Arts Centre Gold Coast (formerly Gold Coast Arts Centre) is a set of facilities that sits on the banks of the Nerang River in the Gold Coast in Queensland, Australia.  A large jetty makes the Arts Centre accessible by Gold Coast Ferries.  The art centre is also home to the Gold Coast City Art Gallery.

Gold Coast railway line | The Gold Coast railway line is an interurban railway line operated by Queensland Rail in Queensland, Australia, connecting Brisbane with the Gold Coast.  This line carries the biggest inter-city commuter traffic in Australia with 26,000 workers commuting each day to Brisbane.
response.summary_2: Sam Weir was born on the Gold Coast, Australia, and the retrieved passages identify that the Gold Coast is the Gold Coast in Queensland, Australia—so they share the same place (the Gold Coast, Queensland).
